bias_defense_notes: Correct-answer positions shuffled across all 5 questions. Distribution: pos0=1, pos1=2, pos2=1, pos3=1. Max share = 2/5 = 0.40 (at boundary, satisfies max_share <= 0.40). All 4 positions used at least once.

questions.objective_ref: Describe what computation means in plain, everyday terms; Give at least two real-world examples of computation happening outside a computer.
Identify the three basic parts of any computation: input, process, and output.
Explain the idea that a computer follows a set of instructions to produce a result.
Distinguish between a task a computer can compute and one it currently cannot.
Identify the three basic parts of any computation: input, process, and output.
